items.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
items.summary: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?

items.title: The Pixel 10a doesn’t have a camera bump, and it’s great
items.summary: The Google Pixel 10a can lay flat on a table, but otherwise the company hasn't brought a ton of upgrades to its newest budget smartphone.

items.title: YouTube CEO says the best YouTubers will ‘never leave their home’
items.summary: CEO Neal Mohan insisted that he isn’t worried about Netflix luring away YouTube's most popular creators.

items.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ becomes Amazon MGM’s biggest box office hit
items.summary: Amazon’s bet on “Project Hail Mary” has paid off handsomely.

items.title: Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment for AI video
items.summary: Is this just normal corporate strategy, or are we about to see a broader pullback on AI-generated video?

items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg texted Elon Musk to offer help with DOGE
items.summary: While the relationship was once thorny enough that Musk challenged Zuckerberg to a cagefight, things seem to have warmed up by the early days of the second Trump administration.

items.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
items.summary: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

items.summary: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
items.summary: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

items.title: What will power the grid in 2035? The race is wide open
items.summary: Fusion, fission, and even natural gas are appeared tied in the race to deliver new power to the grid in the early 2030s.

items.title: Whoop’s fitness band is cool. Can it stay cool as the company grows?
items.summary: Whoop founder Will Ahmed has spent 14 years building a health wearable beloved by elite athletes, and is now racing Oura — and the FDA, and the limits of consumer medicine — to turn it into something that could one day save your life.

items.title: Physical Intelligence is reportedly in talks to raise $1 billion, again
items.summary: The deal would effectively double the company's $5.6 billion valuation in just four months.

items.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
items.summary: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

items.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
items.summary: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.
